refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation communication quality such that packet loss does not exceed the customer-defined limit under agreed network conditions.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the original FR clarifies the performance metric (packet loss) and ties it explicitly to customer-defined limits and network conditions, ensuring measurable and testable requirements.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure packet loss percentage during teleoperation sessions under controlled network conditions and verify compliance with customer-defined limits.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor real-time packet loss during teleoperation.
FR2: Adapt communication parameters to maintain packet loss within limits.
FR3: Report packet loss status to relevant system components.

candidate_dps: DP1: Implement robust error correction and packet retransmission protocols.
DP2: Use adaptive bitrate and compression algorithms to optimize data flow.
DP3: Prioritize teleoperation data packets over less critical data.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Implement robust error correction and packet retransmission protocols.

coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on communication protocol improvements that logically map to the FR without introducing cross-domain coupling.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions are within agreed parameters.
Risk that error correction overhead may increase latency affecting teleoperation responsiveness.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating adaptive bitrate (DP2) if packet loss remains high despite error correction.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Software module responsible for teleoperation communication management and protocol handling.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface for sending and receiving teleoperation data packets over the network.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Interface to report communication quality metrics including packet loss.

module_containment_scores.notes: Broad scope; contains teleoperation but also other functions, leading to moderate diffusion.
Physical vehicle module; no direct containment of teleoperation communication logic.
Primary module for teleoperation communication; highest containment score and minimal diffusion.
May receive safety alerts related to communication but not responsible for teleoperation communication.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Assigning the DP to the Teleoperation module centralizes communication protocol management, minimizing diffusion and improving maintainability.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on Network Link interface stability.
Risk of insufficient integration with Safety Services for safety-critical alerts.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Ensure clear interface contracts with Safety Services and Network Link to mitigate integration risks.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP logically satisfies the refined FR with clear mapping and no coupling issues.

diffusion_risks: Minimal diffusion observed; DP is well contained within Teleoperation module.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores support assignment to Teleoperation module, reducing complexity.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification via telemetry data and network monitoring is feasible.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of responsibility to Teleoperation module with defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Review integration points with Safety Services for safety-critical communication alerts.
Monitor latency impact of error correction protocols on teleoperation responsiveness.

decision.justification: The functional decomposition level is complete with clear FR refinement, DP selection, and IM assignment. All gates pass with manageable risks and verification feasibility.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor real-time packet loss during teleoperation.
FR2: Adapt communication parameters to maintain packet loss within limits.
FR3: Report packet loss status to relevant system components.

revise.why: No immediate revisions required; proceed to next decomposition level.

assumptions: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Teleoperation module can implement required communication protocols.
open_risks: Latency impact of error correction on teleoperation responsiveness.
Integration with Safety Services for safety-critical alerts.
evidence_needed: Telemetry data confirming packet loss within limits under test conditions.
Performance metrics on latency and responsiveness during teleoperation.
